title: Spec: TDMA frame structure and slot assignment
description:
Define TDMA frame structure and slot assignment protocol.

## Location
Add new section to spec/02-physical-link.md or new spec/02a-tdma.md

## Frame structure

### Superframe
- Beacon slot (gateway TX)
- N data slots (node TX)
- Contention slot (new nodes, retries)

### Timing
- Slot duration: airtime of max packet + guard time
- At SF10/125kHz, 60-byte packet: ~200ms + 50ms guard = 250ms slot
- 4 slots/sec, 240 slots/min

### Beacon content
- Superframe number (for sync)
- Slot assignments (compressed bitmap or list)
- Next beacon time

## Slot assignment

### Static assignment
- Node IID hash mod N_SLOTS
- Simple, no coordination, but collisions if hash collides

### Dynamic assignment
- Gateway tracks active nodes
- Assigns slots in beacon or DIO option
- Reassigns on node join/leave

## Guard time
- Compensates for clock drift between beacons
- 50ms guard allows ~1% drift over 5-second superframe
notes:
## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes: Ignore beacons, use ALOHA/CSMA (current behavior)
- New nodes: Sync to beacon, use assigned slots
- Mixed network: Contention slot reserved for old nodes and new-node retries

**Requirements:**
- Beacon MUST be distinguishable from regular traffic (frame type)
- Old nodes MUST ignore unknown frame types
- Contention slot MUST exist in every superframe for ALOHA traffic
- New nodes MUST accept traffic from old nodes at any time (not just slots)
- Gateway MUST receive during all slots AND contention period

**Frame structure:**
- Beacon slot: gateway TX (old nodes see it as noise, ignore)
- Assigned slots: new nodes only
- Contention slot(s): old nodes + new node fallback

**Degradation:**
- More old nodes = more contention slot usage = less TDMA benefit
- But never worse than pure ALOHA (current state)
